# UC-19.3.7 · Storage Spaces Direct (S2D) Cache Device Health and Predictive Failure SMART Signals

## Description

Surfaces Storage Spaces Direct **cache-tier** (**`Usage=Journal`**) physical wear and predictive-loss signals before capacity mirrors exhaust slack—by correlating **`Get-PhysicalDisk`** **`HealthStatus`**/**`OperationalStatus`** transitions (**`Predictive Failure`**, **`Lost Communication`**, **`IO Error`**, **`Stale Metadata`**), **`Get-StorageReliabilityCounter`** NVMe-style metrics (**`PercentageUsed`**, **`Wear`**, **`AvailableSpare`**, **`MediaErrors`**), **Storage Health Service** fault codes (**`STORAGE_HEALTH_FAULT_PHYSICALDISK_PREDICTIVEFAILURE`** / **`_IOERROR`** / **`_LOSTCOMMUNICATION`**), **`Microsoft-Windows-StorageSpaces-Driver/Operational`** events (**304–306**, **207**), **`Microsoft-Windows-Storage-Storport/Admin`** SCSI stress (**153**, **504**, **100**), and **`Perfmon:PhysicalDisk`** hotspots so operators replace **Journal** SSDs/NVMes during staffed windows instead of mid-rebuild scrambles.

## Value

Cache drives absorb bursty writes for **Azure Stack HCI** and **Windows Server S2D** pools—when those flash carriers thin out spare blocks or trip predictive alarms, mirrored spaces still look healthy until a second fault stalls **Rebuild**/**Repair** cadence and guest I/O stalls. Combining **SMART-style** counters with **Storage Health Service** faults and driver event IDs gives storage teams enough runway to stage carrier swaps, validate **firmware**, and rehearse **Get-StorageJob** timelines without surprise data evacuations.

## Implementation

Deploy **Splunk_TA_windows** inputs for the Storage Spaces and Storport channels, add **perfmon** **PhysicalDisk** counters, schedule **`s2d_health_collector.ps1`** every five minutes, and stream **Get-HealthFault** JSON through **HEC**; map **cluster_key** macros to **Failover Cluster** inventory so alerts roll up per **S2D** fleet.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Universal Forwarder on each Azure Stack HCI 23H2 node (GA April 2026) and on Windows Server 2025/2022/2019 Datacenter members running Storage Spaces Direct—agents must remain online while **Get-PhysicalDisk** and **Get-StorageReliabilityCounter** scripted pulls execute under the Storage module.
• **Splunk_TA_windows** ([Splunkbase 742](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)) keeps **WinEventLog:** sourcetypes coherent; target index **storage_s2d** (also mirror wineventlog/windows only when governance splits streams—avoid duplicate EVTX tails).
• inputs.conf stanzas: **[WinEventLog://Microsoft-Windows-StorageSpaces-Driver/Operational]**, **[WinEventLog://Microsoft-Windows-Storage-Storport/Admin]**, **[WinEventLog://Microsoft-Windows-StorageSpaces-ManagementAgent/Operational]**, **[perfmon://PhysicalDisk]** with counters % Disk Time, Avg. Disk Queue Length, Disk Bytes/sec, plus **[script://.\bin\s2d_health_collector.ps1]** (interval = 300, **index = storage_s2d**, **sourcetype = s2d:reliability**) emitting **Get-PhysicalDisk | Get-StorageReliabilityCounter | ConvertTo-Json** output so Wear, PercentageUsed, AvailableSpare, AvailableSpareThreshold, CriticalWarning, MediaErrors, ReadErrorsUncorrected, and WriteErrorsUncorrected land as structured JSON.
• HEC token for **Get-StorageSubSystem | Get-HealthFault | ConvertTo-Json** into **sourcetype=s2d:healthfault** and optional **Get-PhysicalDisk** inventory snapshots tagged **sourcetype=s2d:physicaldisk** (serialize Usage, OperationalStatus, HealthStatus, BusType, FirmwareVersion, SerialNumber).
• RBAC: roles need **storage_s2d**, wineventlog, and performance indexes authorized for **hci_storage_ops** dashboards.

Step 1 — Configure collection around Journal cache carriers
(1) Verify **Get-PhysicalDisk** lists Usage=Journal only on NVMe/SATA SSD cache devices while capacity HDDs stay Usage=AutoSelect; cross-check **Get-StorageBusBinding** so scripted filters never confuse **Manual-Select** lab disks with production Journal rows.
(2) Pair **Get-StorageReliabilityCounter** with **Get-CimInstance** **MSFT_PhysicalDisk** snapshots (**Root\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Storage**) capturing OperationalStatus values such as Predictive Failure, Lost Communication, IO Error, Stale Metadata, and HealthStatus enums Healthy/Warning/Unhealthy before JSON export.
(3) Retain **Microsoft-Windows-StorageSpaces-Driver/Operational** events long enough to study 304 cache-bound, 305 cache-unbound, 306 cache-rebind, and 207 disk-marked-failed alongside **Get-StorageJob** tasks named Rebuild, Repair, Regeneration, or Reallocation.
(4) Keep **Microsoft-Windows-Storage-Storport/Admin** 153/504/100 bursts plus System 7/11/51/153 disk/partmgr signatures; annotate maintenance windows so enclosure firmware drives benign SCSI resets that lack spaces_drv corroboration stay annotated in CMDB.
(5) When **Debug-StorageSubSystem** runs, archive transcripts offline—Splunk ingests only compact **Get-HealthFault** JSON referencing subsystem FriendlyName values such as Clustered Windows Storage on \<ClusterName\>.

Step 2 — Promote correlation SPL after TA field checks
Paste the packaged spl field once EventCode versus event_id aliasing matches your TA build.

```spl
(index=storage_s2d OR index=windows OR index=wineventlog OR index=azure_stack_hci OR index=hci_cluster)
(`sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-StorageSpaces-Driver/Operational"` OR `sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Storage-Storport/Admin"` OR `sourcetype="WinEventLog:System"` OR sourcetype="s2d:reliability" OR sourcetype="s2d:healthfault" OR sourcetype="s2d:physicaldisk" OR sourcetype="Perfmon:PhysicalDisk")
earliest=-24h
| eval evt_code=tostring(coalesce(EventCode, EventID, event_id, ""))
| eval evt_num=tonumber(evt_code)
| eval spaces_drv=case(evt_num IN (304,305,306,207), evt_num, null())
| eval storport_hit=case(evt_num IN (153,504,100), evt_num, null())
| eval system_disk=sourcetype=="WinEventLog:System" AND evt_num IN (7,11,51,153)
| eval wear_pct=tonumber(coalesce(PercentageUsed, Wear, percentage_used, ""))
| eval spare_warn=isnotnull(AvailableSpare) AND isnotnull(AvailableSpareThreshold) AND tonumber(AvailableSpare)<=tonumber(AvailableSpareThreshold)
| eval critical_warn_low=sourcetype=="s2d:reliability" AND spare_warn
| eval uncorrected_io=sourcetype=="s2d:reliability" AND (tonumber(ReadErrorsUncorrected)>0 OR tonumber(WriteErrorsUncorrected)>0 OR tonumber(MediaErrors)>0)
| eval wear_gate=sourcetype=="s2d:reliability" AND wear_pct>=75
| eval health_pf=sourcetype=="s2d:healthfault" AND match(lower(_raw), "(?i)storage_health_fault_physicaldisk_predictivefailure")
| eval pd_journal_bad=sourcetype=="s2d:physicaldisk" AND match(lower(coalesce(Usage,"")), "(?i)journal") AND match(lower(coalesce(OperationalStatus,"")), "(?i)predictive failure|lost communication|io error|degraded")
| eval pf_txt=sourcetype!="Perfmon:PhysicalDisk" AND match(lower(Message), "(?i)predictive failure|lost communication")
| where isnotnull(spaces_drv) OR isnotnull(storport_hit) OR system_disk OR critical_warn_low OR uncorrected_io OR wear_gate OR health_pf OR pd_journal_bad OR pf_txt
| eval cluster_key=upper(trim(coalesce(Cluster_Name, cluster_name, fq_cluster, ComputerName, host)))
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
        values(evt_code) as win_event_codes,
        values(spaces_drv) as driver_evt_ids,
        values(storport_hit) as storport_evt_ids,
        max(wear_pct) as peak_wear_pct,
        dc(host) as reporting_nodes
    by cluster_key
| eval posture=case(
    peak_wear_pct>=80 OR mvfind(driver_evt_ids,"207")>=0 OR mvfind(driver_evt_ids,"304")>=0, "predictive_focus",
    mvfind(driver_evt_ids,"305")>=0 OR mvfind(driver_evt_ids,"306")>=0, "cache_bind_noise",
    peak_wear_pct>=60 OR mvfind(storport_evt_ids,"153")>=0 OR mvfind(storport_evt_ids,"100")>=0, "investigate_physical",
    1=1, "mixed_signals")
| sort -last_seen
| table cluster_key, posture, peak_wear_pct, driver_evt_ids, storport_evt_ids, win_event_codes, reporting_nodes, last_seen
```

**Search logic:**
– spaces_drv highlights 304/305/306/207 sequences tied to cache-tier churn inside **Microsoft-Windows-StorageSpaces-Driver/Operational**.
– wear_pct merges PercentageUsed NVMe wear-leveling counters with Wear fields returned from **Get-StorageReliabilityCounter** for mixed OEM fleets.
– health_pf anchors **STORAGE_HEALTH_FAULT_PHYSICALDISK_PREDICTIVEFAILURE** strings delivered through HEC without relying solely on localized Message text.
– mvfind keeps posture stable when stats aggregates multi-valued event codes during rolling drain windows.

Throttle predictive_focus alerts per SerialNumber for four hours after Replace Physical Disk tickets attach approved maintenance metadata in KV lookups.

Step 3 — Validate against PowerShell consoles
(1) On suspect nodes run **Get-PhysicalDisk | Where-Object Usage -eq Journal | Format-Table FriendlyName,SerialNumber,HealthStatus,OperationalStatus,BusType,IsPartial** and compare timestamps to Splunk last_seen—drift hints forwarder clock skew needing w32tm remediation.
(2) Export **Get-StorageReliabilityCounter -PhysicalDisk $disk -Detailed** focusing on PercentageUsed nearing OEM replacement guidance (often 80%) and decode CriticalWarning bits (AvailableSpare below threshold, temperature excursions, read-only transitions, volatile backup failures).
(3) Compare live **Get-StorageSubSystem | Get-HealthFault** output to **s2d:healthfault** rows; confirm fault codes such as **STORAGE_HEALTH_FAULT_PHYSICALDISK_IOERROR** and **STORAGE_HEALTH_FAULT_PHYSICALDISK_LOSTCOMMUNICATION** match JSON escaping rules.
(4) Use Windows Admin Center (2410+) Storage Spaces Direct blade to confirm Journal rebalance activity aligns with 306 rebinding spikes visible in Splunk.
(5) Overlay **Perfmon:PhysicalDisk** Avg. Disk Queue Length with storport 153 resets—concordance strengthens narratives linking SCSI stress to Lost Communication without contradictory Wear trends.

Step 4 — Operationalise dashboards and paging
(1) Publish predictive_focus posture tiles with peak_wear_pct gauges per **cluster_key** plus driver_evt_ids multi-select drilldowns sourced from **storage_s2d** ACL-safe macros.
(2) Layer Perfmon % Disk Time heatmaps whenever storport_evt_ids show 504 unit-attention clusters—colour bands separate maintenance-tagged drains from unexplained resets.
(3) Keep Storage Replica throughput ribbons optional context-only; capacity replication depth belongs to sibling monitors while this view stays on PhysicalDisk cache fatigue.
(4) Show **Test-Cluster** storage_bus_cache ratio imports beside cache_bind_noise posture so operators distinguish **Get-StorageBusCache** scheduler skew from SMART cliffs.
(5) Require ticket webhook enrichment (**hci_drive_swap**) to auto-suppress pages until **Get-PhysicalDisk** returns OperationalStatus=OK with refreshed FirmwareVersion after swaps complete.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting misleading modes

• Quiet scripted sourcetype — splunkd.log shows no TailReader progress for **s2d:reliability** when PowerShell buffers stdout; add [Console]::Out.Flush() after each JSON payload and absolute **powershell.exe -File** paths in inputs.conf.

• Blank Wear fields — some firmware exposes Wear as zero while PercentageUsed climbs; extend coalesce() chains after fieldsummary reviews of spath extractions from **Get-StorageReliabilityCounter -Detailed**.

• NETBIOS vs physical host names — deduplicate **cluster_key** using nightly **Get-Cluster** dumps stored in lookup **hci_cluster_hosts.csv** so duplicate nodes never inflate reporting_nodes.

• Missing CriticalWarning — until vendor SES feeds arrive, manually compare CriticalWarning bitmask transitions with OperationalStatus flips gathered through **Get-CimInstance** when Splunk JSON lacks vendor extensions.

• Maintenance drains — suppress predictive_focus while MaintenanceRecords marks storage_maintenance=true for **Invoke-StorageMaintenance** or Azure Stack HCI lifecycle actions that intentionally evacuate Journal devices.

• Firmware waves — planned NVMe firmware activation can emit Predictive Failure chatter until activation completes; gate pages on **Cluster-Aware Updating** job tags (**journal_firmware_wave**) so half-updated carriers do not page alone.

• **Capacity-disk replacements** — adding new HDD shelves forces Journal rebinding (305/306) without wear issues; require Wear/PercentageUsed confirmation before treating bind noise as impending failure.

• Enclosure firmware SCSI resets — transient 153 storms during backplane updates mimic I/O loss; demand spaces_drv 304/207 correlation or **Get-HealthFault** LOSTCOMMUNICATION before treating as drive failure.

• Vendor wear CLI vs Microsoft — OEM utilities may report 15–25 point higher wear percentages than **Get-StorageReliabilityCounter**; maintain lookup oem_vs_ms_wear or standardize on one instrumentation path to avoid contradictory tickets.

• **Hyper-V migration spikes** — **Perfmon:PhysicalDisk** saturation during live migration can resemble cache starvation; pair Disk Bytes/sec bursts with predictive_focus posture and storport 100 sense data before blaming Journal SSDs.

• Virtual disk redundancy messaging — if **STORAGE_HEALTH_FAULT_VIRTUALDISK_NOREDUNDANCY** appears, escalate through storage SME channels—this UC focuses on PhysicalDisk cache wear yet those faults often co-travel during dual failures.

• JSON field collisions — when **ConvertTo-Json** nests arrays, spath may miss MediaErrors; fall back to rex macros tuned for your schema until **MSFT_PhysicalDisk** field names stabilize across OS builds.


## SPL

```spl
(index=storage_s2d OR index=windows OR index=wineventlog OR index=azure_stack_hci OR index=hci_cluster)
(`sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-StorageSpaces-Driver/Operational"` OR `sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Storage-Storport/Admin"` OR `sourcetype="WinEventLog:System"` OR sourcetype="s2d:reliability" OR sourcetype="s2d:healthfault" OR sourcetype="s2d:physicaldisk" OR sourcetype="Perfmon:PhysicalDisk")
earliest=-24h
| eval evt_code=tostring(coalesce(EventCode, EventID, event_id, ""))
| eval evt_num=tonumber(evt_code)
| eval spaces_drv=case(evt_num IN (304,305,306,207), evt_num, null())
| eval storport_hit=case(evt_num IN (153,504,100), evt_num, null())
| eval system_disk=sourcetype=="WinEventLog:System" AND evt_num IN (7,11,51,153)
| eval wear_pct=tonumber(coalesce(PercentageUsed, Wear, percentage_used, ""))
| eval spare_warn=isnotnull(AvailableSpare) AND isnotnull(AvailableSpareThreshold) AND tonumber(AvailableSpare)<=tonumber(AvailableSpareThreshold)
| eval critical_warn_low=sourcetype=="s2d:reliability" AND spare_warn
| eval uncorrected_io=sourcetype=="s2d:reliability" AND (tonumber(ReadErrorsUncorrected)>0 OR tonumber(WriteErrorsUncorrected)>0 OR tonumber(MediaErrors)>0)
| eval wear_gate=sourcetype=="s2d:reliability" AND wear_pct>=75
| eval health_pf=sourcetype=="s2d:healthfault" AND match(lower(_raw), "(?i)storage_health_fault_physicaldisk_predictivefailure")
| eval pd_journal_bad=sourcetype=="s2d:physicaldisk" AND match(lower(coalesce(Usage,"")), "(?i)journal") AND match(lower(coalesce(OperationalStatus,"")), "(?i)predictive failure|lost communication|io error|degraded")
| eval pf_txt=sourcetype!="Perfmon:PhysicalDisk" AND match(lower(Message), "(?i)predictive failure|lost communication")
| where isnotnull(spaces_drv) OR isnotnull(storport_hit) OR system_disk OR critical_warn_low OR uncorrected_io OR wear_gate OR health_pf OR pd_journal_bad OR pf_txt
| eval cluster_key=upper(trim(coalesce(Cluster_Name, cluster_name, fq_cluster, ComputerName, host)))
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
        values(evt_code) as win_event_codes,
        values(spaces_drv) as driver_evt_ids,
        values(storport_hit) as storport_evt_ids,
        max(wear_pct) as peak_wear_pct,
        dc(host) as reporting_nodes
    by cluster_key
| eval posture=case(
    peak_wear_pct>=80 OR mvfind(driver_evt_ids,"207")>=0 OR mvfind(driver_evt_ids,"304")>=0, "predictive_focus",
    mvfind(driver_evt_ids,"305")>=0 OR mvfind(driver_evt_ids,"306")>=0, "cache_bind_noise",
    peak_wear_pct>=60 OR mvfind(storport_evt_ids,"153")>=0 OR mvfind(storport_evt_ids,"100")>=0, "investigate_physical",
    1=1, "mixed_signals")
| sort -last_seen
| table cluster_key, posture, peak_wear_pct, driver_evt_ids, storport_evt_ids, win_event_codes, reporting_nodes, last_seen
```

## Visualization

(1) **Posture tiles** contrasting **predictive_focus** versus **cache_bind_noise** over 24h. (2) **Timechart** of **peak_wear_pct** per **cluster_key** with reference line at **80%** **PercentageUsed**. (3) **Table** listing **driver_evt_ids**, **storport_evt_ids**, **win_event_codes**, **reporting_nodes**, **last_seen**. (4) **Heatmap** pairing **Perfmon** **Avg. Disk Queue Length** with **storport** **153** counts. (5) **Drill panel** tying **SerialNumber** from **s2d:physicaldisk** to CMDB carrier records and live **Get-PhysicalDisk** screenshots.
